_instructions: For each app, open evidence_url (or search for the real docs if it's missing/broken) and fill in verdict.<field> with one of ('correct', 'incorrect', 'unsure'). Add a one-line correction in verdict_notes.<field> when marking incorrect. Leave a field's verdict as null if you skip it (it will be excluded from scoring, not counted as wrong).

claimed.api_surface: Wrapped by Composio as 99 agent-callable tools (managed via Composio's toolkit catalog for 'attio')
claimed.buildability_verdict: Yes - already an agent toolkit today via Composio

verdict_notes.auth_method: Attio supports API key (primary self-serve method, generated in Workspace Settings > Developers) AND OAuth2 for published apps. Claim only lists OAuth, missing the API key path most self-serve devs actually use.
verdict_notes.self_serve_or_gated: Confirmed: any workspace admin can generate an API key directly, free tier 1,000 req/month, no approval needed.
verdict_notes.api_surface: Can't independently verify the exact '99 tools' count without Composio catalog access.
verdict_notes.has_mcp: Attio has an official MCP server (docs.attio.com/mcp) confirmed via developer platform page.
verdict_notes.buildability_verdict: Confirmed buildable — real self-serve API key auth plus official MCP.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: attio.com is the marketing homepage. Real docs are at developers.attio.com / docs.attio.com.

claimed.api_surface: standardized APIs and SDKs for location data and services
claimed.buildability_verdict: Insufficient direct documentation to confirm buildability as a Composio agent toolkit
claimed.blocker: No explicit documentation on authentication, API access model, or developer onboarding

verdict_notes.auth_method: Ungrounded — evidence_url doesn't even resolve to Twenty's site.
verdict_notes.self_serve_or_gated: Agent honestly flagged 'unknown' given it had no real evidence; can't score as correct/incorrect since nothing was actually checked.
verdict_notes.api_surface: Claimed 'standardized APIs and SDKs for location data and services' — that's HERE.com's API, not Twenty CRM at all. Fully ungrounded hallucination from a bad source page.
verdict_notes.has_mcp: No real evidence gathered either way.
verdict_notes.buildability_verdict: Agent's own verdict correctly flagged insufficient documentation rather than fabricating confidence.
verdict_notes.blocker: Accurately described as lacking direct documentation — matches what happened.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: This is a Bing ad-click redirect to a doubleclick.net tracking link, not a Twenty CRM page at all.

claimed.buildability_verdict: Yes - already an agent toolkit today via Composio

verdict_notes.auth_method: Intercom supports OAuth2 and API keys per developers.intercom.com.
verdict_notes.api_surface: Documented public REST API, moderate-broad — matches developers.intercom.com.
verdict_notes.has_mcp: Intercom has an official MCP server.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: developers.intercom.com is the real, correct docs root.

claimed.api_surface: Wrapped by Composio as 0 agent-callable tools (managed via Composio's toolkit catalog for 'front')
claimed.buildability_verdict: Yes - already an agent toolkit today via Composio

verdict_notes.auth_method: Front supports BOTH API tokens and OAuth2 (OAuth required for public/partner integrations). Claim only lists API key, omitting OAuth entirely.
verdict_notes.self_serve_or_gated: Confirmed: free developer account at dev.frontapp.com, API token self-serve in minutes.
verdict_notes.api_surface: Can't verify Composio's exact tool count independently.
verdict_notes.has_mcp: Front's own docs confirm an MCP server offering ('MCP server allows you to connect Front data... to clients like Claude, ChatGPT').
verdict_notes.buildability_verdict: Internal contradiction: api_surface says the Composio toolkit has 0 agent-callable tools, yet buildability_verdict says 'Yes - already an agent toolkit today.' Zero tools does not support that verdict.
verdict_notes.blocker: Should not be 'none' if the Composio toolkit genuinely has 0 tools — that itself is the blocker (empty/stub toolkit) worth surfacing, not glossing over.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: frontapp.com is the marketing homepage. Real docs: dev.frontapp.com.

claimed.api_surface: Wrapped by Composio as 12 agent-callable tools (managed via Composio's toolkit catalog for 'pylon_mcp')
claimed.buildability_verdict: Yes - already an agent toolkit today via Composio

verdict_notes.auth_method: 'Dynamic Client Registration OAuth' is a legitimate, specific pattern for native MCP OAuth flows and matches Pylon's MCP-first design.
verdict_notes.self_serve_or_gated: No evidence of a gate; Pylon MCP is publicly documented.
verdict_notes.api_surface: Can't independently verify the '12 tools' figure.
verdict_notes.has_mcp: Confirmed by evidence_url itself — mcp.usepylon.com is Pylon's real, dedicated MCP subdomain.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: mcp.usepylon.com is a real, directly relevant URL — this is one of the few Composio rows where evidence isn't just the homepage.

claimed.api_surface: Wrapped by Composio as 129 agent-callable tools (managed via Composio's toolkit catalog for 'help_scout')
claimed.buildability_verdict: Yes - already an agent toolkit today via Composio

verdict_notes.auth_method: Help Scout's Mailbox API uses OAuth2.
verdict_notes.self_serve_or_gated: Free developer account, well-documented self-serve OAuth app creation.
verdict_notes.buildability_verdict: Consistent with self-serve OAuth + documented API.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: helpscout.com is the marketing homepage. Real docs: developer.helpscout.com.

claimed.api_surface: Wrapped by Composio as 57 agent-callable tools (managed via Composio's toolkit catalog for 'whatsapp')
claimed.buildability_verdict: Yes - already an agent toolkit today via Composio

verdict_notes.auth_method: WhatsApp Business Platform primarily uses system-user access tokens issued via Meta Business Manager, not a standard OAuth2 consumer flow — 'OAuth2' is an oversimplification.
verdict_notes.self_serve_or_gated: Confirmed gated: requires Meta Business Verification, WhatsApp-specific app review for solution providers, phone number registration, and message-template approval before production use. Not self-serve.
verdict_notes.buildability_verdict: Composio may indeed offer a toolkit, but calling it flatly buildable glosses over the real approval gate end users must clear.
verdict_notes.blocker: Should not be 'none' — Meta Business Verification + template approval is a real, documented blocker for production use.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: web.whatsapp.com is the consumer web client, not developer docs. Real docs: developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp.

claimed.api_surface: REST API with endpoints for Users, Teams, Calls, Numbers, Messages, Contacts, Tags, Webhooks, Companies, Integrations, Participants, IVR Options, AI Voice Agent, Analytics, and Mobile features
claimed.buildability_verdict: Aircall provides a comprehensive, authenticated REST API with OAuth 2.0 for partners and Basic Auth for customers, supporting broad functionality and webhook event subscriptions, enabling building a Composio agent toolkit integration.

verdict_notes.auth_method: Aircall API docs confirm OAuth2 (partners) and Basic Auth (customers).
verdict_notes.self_serve_or_gated: Aircall gates OAuth partner integrations behind a review process; matches 'gated' verdict.
verdict_notes.api_surface: Matches the broad endpoint list on developer.aircall.io.
verdict_notes.buildability_verdict: Reasonable given a real documented REST API exists.
verdict_notes.blocker: Internal contradiction — blocker says 'no public API docs found' while evidence_url points straight at developer.aircall.io/api-references, which IS public API docs. These two fields disagree with each other.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: developer.aircall.io/api-references is a real, relevant docs page.

claimed.buildability_verdict: Unknown - docs page had insufficient text

verdict_notes.auth_method: Null — not scored, agent correctly declined to guess.
verdict_notes.buildability_verdict: Honestly reported as unknown rather than fabricated — this is the right failure behavior.
verdict_notes.blocker: Accurately describes a JS-rendered docs page defeating the fetcher — this is a genuine, honestly-reported failure mode worth keeping in the write-up.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: developer.vonage.com/en/documentation is the real docs root, even though the fetcher couldn't extract text from it.

claimed.auth_method: Meta Marketing Api Oauth (also Meta Ads Api Key)
claimed.api_surface: Wrapped by Composio as 50 agent-callable tools (managed via Composio's toolkit catalog for 'metaads')
claimed.buildability_verdict: Yes - already an agent toolkit today via Composio

verdict_notes.auth_method: Meta Marketing API uses OAuth2 (Ads Management Standard Access) plus long-lived tokens; matches claim.
verdict_notes.self_serve_or_gated: Self-serve for testing on your own ad account, but production/scaled use requires App Review and Business Verification — the flat 'self-serve' label glosses over this.
verdict_notes.buildability_verdict: Reasonable for own-account use case, though production-scale caveat should be surfaced.
verdict_notes.blocker: Should note the App Review / Business Verification requirement for anything beyond your own ad account — that's a real, documented gate.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: business.meta.com is a product marketing page, not developer docs. Real docs: developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-apis.

claimed.auth_method: client_id and secret via request body or headers
claimed.api_surface: JSON over HTTP POST endpoints with webhooks, including sandbox and production environments
claimed.buildability_verdict: Plaid provides a comprehensive authenticated API with sandbox and production environments, client libraries, and OpenAPI specs, suitable for building a Composio agent toolkit integration.

verdict_notes.auth_method: client_id/secret is accurate for Plaid's API auth.
verdict_notes.self_serve_or_gated: Oversimplified: Plaid's Sandbox environment is instantly self-serve and free with no approval. Only Production access requires an application/review. Flatly labeling it 'gated' misses the real self-serve sandbox path, which matters for a buildability verdict.
verdict_notes.buildability_verdict: Reasonable given a real, well-documented API with sandbox access.
verdict_notes.blocker: Correctly left as no fixed blocker given sandbox self-serve exists.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: plaid.com/docs/api/ is real, correct developer documentation.

claimed.buildability_verdict: Yes - already an agent toolkit today via Composio

verdict_notes.auth_method: Xero uses OAuth2 exclusively for its API — matches claim (no separate API-key path, which is itself worth noting since the claim implies it's the only method, which is accurate).
verdict_notes.self_serve_or_gated: Free developer account, self-serve OAuth app registration.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: xero.com is the marketing homepage. Real docs: developer.xero.com.

claimed.auth_method: API key via Authorization header and environment variable REDUCTO_API_KEY
claimed.api_surface: Endpoints for file upload (/upload), document parsing (/parse), data extraction (/extract), document splitting (/split_document), classification (/classify_document), and editing (/edit_document); SDKs in Python, Node.js, Go; CLI and MCP server integration
claimed.buildability_verdict: Reducto provides a comprehensive authenticated API with multiple endpoints and SDKs, plus MCP server support, enabling it to serve as a Composio agent toolkit

verdict_notes.auth_method: API key via Authorization header, matches docs.reducto.ai.
verdict_notes.api_surface: Endpoint list matches Reducto's documented API surface.
verdict_notes.buildability_verdict: Consistent with above, and this is a genuinely clean web-sourced row.
verdict_notes.evidence_url: docs.reducto.ai/quickstart is real, correct, and specific — this web-sourced row is clean end to end.
